title: Unable to use --new for generating systemd service after running play kube
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

I am unable to use the `--new` flag in `podman generate systemd --new` on a pod if it was created using `podman play kube`. Podman is running in rootless mode.

```
Error: cannot use --new on pod "b94ee2cf08d...7d88557397e": no create command found
```

I found this [other sample deployment](https://github.com/jwillikers/unifi-controller) and it does seem to be using the exact same steps without any issues. I am wondering if it's related to the podman version or maybe a regression.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Deploy a container inside a pod with `podman play kube`
2. Run `podman generate systemd --new pod_name` to generate a portable systemd service. If `--new` flag is not used, then this works fine.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.3.1
API Version:  3.3.1
Go Version:   go1.16.7
Built:        Tue Sep 21 10:41:42 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

Podman is running on an on-prem RHEL8, I coudn't try on the latest version of podman.

comments.body:
podman generate systemd --new is only supported for container created by podman run/create, see https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/source/markdown/podman-generate-systemd.1.md#--new

I do not think that your linked example ever worked.
